projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Explore our project gallery where technical excellence meets innovation to deliver exceptional results.

items.name: Centauri Project
items.description: Expansion of the industrial pharmaceutical production plant. Building 2, Powerhouse 5, and service area. The plant is located in Pocito Department, San Juan, Argentina. Total construction area is approximately 4,727.00 m2.
items.brief-description: Pharmaceutical plant expansion — Industrial plant expansion in Pocito, San Juan. 4,727 m².
items.client: Monte Verde S. A. - ADIUM PHARMA

items.name: Provincial Legislature Annex Building
items.description: Construction of a 5,960.00 m² building in a horizontal volume consisting of three basements, ground floor, and four upper levels, plus a fifth service level projected above the existing house as a unique body with pure, straight lines. Service areas connect in the west sector formed by a closed volume with vertical circulations (stairs and elevators) connecting the entire building; in the east sector, a longitudinal courtyard in a north-south direction serves as a bioclimatic regulator. The ground floor and first floor feature auditorium spaces and a library - interactive room.
items.brief-description: Multifunctional building of 5,960 m² with basements, ground floor, five floors, auditorium, and library.

items.name: Los Molinos Neighborhood
items.description: Neighborhood of 260 functional units in towers of two and three floors, five prototypes with traditional construction system, foundation slab, brickwork, reinforced concrete slabs, aluminum and wood carpentry, ceramic floors in bathrooms and ceramic wall tiles in bathrooms and kitchens, painted with latex on exterior and interior walls and synthetic enamel on wood carpentry.
items.brief-description: Residential neighborhood of 260 homes in 2 and 3-story towers.

items.name: Gran Tulum Aqueduct
items.description: Installation of stainless steel piping, water treatment plant, and HDPE piping. Carried out by UTE: Sigma, Mapal, and Benito Roggio
items.brief-description: Installation of stainless steel piping, water treatment plant, and HDPE piping.

items.name: Ullum Solar Transformer Station 132/33 kV
items.description: 132kv high-voltage line interconnecting Albardón S.E. with Ullúm Solar S.E., with a total length of 18km. The work allows incorporating 180MW of solar energy generated by the Ullum Alfa and El Chaguar parks into the electrical system. Work executed along with Conectar S.R.L.
items.brief-description: 18 km power line incorporating 180 MW of solar energy into the grid.

items.name: Commercial Gallery - Ayres Village
items.description: Construction of a commercial building developed on 2 floors. The typology is a Strip Mall with a semi-covered gallery across its entire front serving as a transition and entrance space for the commercial premises. The complex has 20 commercial spaces ranging in surface area between 45.00 m² and 160.00 m². The building consists of mixed systems including traditional masonry, frames, metal trusses, lightweight panels, reinforced concrete slabs, and steel frame system.
items.brief-description: Strip Mall — 2-story commercial building with 20 premises and semi-covered gallery.

items.name: Ayres Village Neighborhood
items.description: Urbanization of 77 lots and construction of 12 3-bedroom houses, each 150.96 m², with a total Area = 1,811.52 m², Sewage Network, Drinking Water Networks, Paved Streets, Concrete Sidewalks, Landscaping of green spaces, Lighting, Underground electrical wiring, Natural Gas network. Total land area 49,934.88 m²
items.brief-description: Urbanization of 77 lots with 12 3-bedroom houses and full infrastructure.

items.name: Ayres Libertador Neighborhood
items.description: Urbanization of 106 lots and construction of 42 155 m² houses with a total Area = 6,510 m², Sewage Network, Drinking Water Networks, Paved Streets, Concrete Sidewalks, Landscaping of green spaces, Lighting, Underground electrical wiring, Natural Gas network. Total land area 10,103.80 m²
items.brief-description: 106 lots and 42 155 m² houses with full infrastructure: sewage, water, gas, paving, lighting, and underground wiring.

items.name: Ayres Del Este Private Neighborhood
items.description: Construction of 120 2 and 3 bedroom houses Area: 9,320 m² and 10 duplexes Area: 700 m² of top quality, Aluminum carpentry, porcelain floors, high-end finishes, water network, sewage network, gas network, pressurized irrigation of green spaces and trees, underground electrical network, entrance portal, paved streets, concrete sidewalks, green spaces, IT communication system, etc.
items.brief-description: 120 2 and 3-bedroom houses and 10 premium duplexes, with full infrastructure, vehicular access, green spaces, and IT system.

items.name: Project Access Road Maintenance
items.description: Opening, maintenance, and repair services for the Access Road to the Azules mining project, ensuring safe transit and normal execution of exploration project activities. Snow and/or ice clearance, stabilization and repair of road side slopes, construction and/or replacement of safety berms, construction and/or maintenance of escape ramps for emergency exits, cleaning and removal of material from large landslides, collapses, avalanches and/or floods, repair, maintenance and/or construction of mandatory rest or stopping areas, etc.
items.brief-description: Maintenance and repair of the access road to the Azules mining project: snow clearance, slope stabilization, safety berms, emergency ramps, and landslide cleanup.

items.name: Mining Service
items.description:
Open-pit mining operation in the Sánchez, Norte, and Magnata pits, over a 24-month period (2025–2027). It is expected to move 3.40 million tons. 
The service includes: mobilization, demobilization, pre-production mining activities at the Hualilán deposits, mineral stockpile platform loading and management, cleaning and clearing of vegetation layer, loading, hauling and transport to stockpile platforms, waste management in dumps, development and maintenance of internal and access roads, dust control, drainage and water management, among others.
items.brief-description: Open-pit mining at Sánchez, Norte, and Magnata pits for 24 months (2025–2027), moving 3.4 million tons, including extraction, transport, waste management, and road/water management.

items.name: Dump Preparation
items.description: Construction of a dump at the Lunahuasi mining project located in San Juan province.
items.brief-description: Dump construction at the Lunahuasi mining project.

items.name: Water Intake Design Modification at Las Taguas River – Bocatoma Project
items.description: Design of a new intake work starting from the current system of filtering galleries, settler, and dissipation basin. The goal is to project an intake structure that optimizes available resources and is efficient during low-flow periods, making the most of existing civil works.
items.brief-description: Design of a new water intake from existing filter galleries, optimizing water collection especially during low-flow periods.

items.name: Access Road Maintenance at Veladero Mining Project
items.description: Opening, maintenance, and repair services for the Access Road to the Veladero mine, Lama project and surroundings, to enable safe transit and normal execution of mine-related activities. Snow and/or ice clearance, stabilization and repair of road side slopes, construction and/or replacement of safety berms, construction and/or maintenance of escape ramps for emergency exits, cleaning and removal of material from large landslides, collapses, avalanches and/or floods, repair, maintenance and/or construction of mandatory rest or stopping areas, etc.
items.brief-description: Maintenance and repair of the access road to the Veladero mine and Lama project: snow clearance, slope stabilization, safety berms, emergency ramps, and landslide cleanup.

items.name: Provision and Operation of Auxiliary Equipment for Veladero Mine Operation
items.description: Heavy Equipment Provision and Operation service in all sectors and facilities (Processes, Mina, Winter Operation) within the Veladero Operation and its service dependencies taking the entrance named Garita Veladero as a reference point. In the summer season, there are 36 pieces of equipment on site, which increase during the Winter operation.
items.brief-description: Provision and operation of heavy equipment in all sectors of the Veladero mine, with 36 units in summer and increased capacity in winter.

services.title: Services
services.description: Offering concrete solutions to complex requirements for over 35 years through personalized technical advice for project and construction management.

engineering.title: Project Engineering
engineering.description: We develop engineering projects in their various stages, ensuring efficient, safe technical solutions that align with the requirements of each work.

construction.title: Construction
construction.description: We execute civil and industrial construction projects with high quality standards, optimizing resources and potentalizing the work of our professional team.

real-estate.title: Real Estate Developments
real-estate.description: We focus on the planning, execution, and commercialization of high-quality private neighborhoods, distinguished by urban qualities and strategic locations.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: Implementing civil and architectural works for public and private sectors, covering everything from planning to coordination, ensuring quality and safety.

mining.title: Mining
mining.description: Specialized technical team for executing projects in mining locations with cutting-edge technology for massive soil movements and civil works.

equipment.title: Heavy Equipment Provision
equipment.description: We have our own fleet of equipment to offer provision and operation services in all stages of mining projects with highly qualified personnel.

form.worked_projects: Did you work on any of our projects?
form.cv_note: *Remember to attach your CV in the email
form.error: We couldn't send the message. Please try again later.

history.title: History
history.description: SIGMA S.A. was founded in 1988 with the conviction of developing high-quality construction systems in San Juan and across the country. Since its inception, the company has focused on innovation, incorporating cutting-edge technologies and work techniques to optimize resources and empower a highly trained professional team.

mission.title: Mission
mission.description: Our mission consists of providing engineering and construction services for major civil, industrial, sanitation, and road works in a responsible and sustainable manner within the Province of San Juan.

vision.title: Vision
vision.description: At SIGMA, our vision is to position ourselves as the best and most innovative company in the construction and engineering field and to remain at the forefront of new technologies, based on the specialization and training of our team, while respecting environmental conservation standards.

values.title: Values
values.description: At SIGMA S.A. we act with ethics, honesty, and transparency. We promote teamwork and continuous improvement, caring for the health and safety of our collaborators and respecting the environment through sustainable practices.

policy.title: Quality, Safety, Occupational Health and Environment Policy.
policy.description: At SIGMA we are committed to satisfying the requirements and expectations of our clients and other stakeholders in an efficient, sustainable manner and with the highest standards of quality, safety and environmental care, maintaining an Integrated Management System based on ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015 and ISO 45001:2018 standards.

modal.title: Quality, Safety, Occupational Health and Environment Policy
modal.intro: Fulfilling our vision, we aim to:
points: Innovate and continuously improve our processes, products, and services, guaranteeing high standards of quality, efficiency, and sustainability to establish ourselves as benchmarks in the region.
Value the efforts of our workers, promoting their professional development through constant training, teamwork, consultation, and participation of workers and their representatives.
Develop innovative solutions that generate mutual benefits for clients, workers, the community, and the environment.
Prevent and mitigate occupational risks, eliminating hazards and reducing risks to the safety and health of our collaborators.
Protect the environment by preventing pollution and promoting the sustainable use of natural resources.
Act with ethics, transparency, and responsibility, complying with legal and other applicable requirements.
Continuously improve the effectiveness and performance of our Integrated Management System.
modal.footer: SIGMA Management assumes these commitments under the responsibility of being accountable for their performance, through monitoring, measurement, and review of their management system.
